title: The City: Location & Articles
titleFr: La ville : il y a et les articles
cefrDescriptors: Can say what there is in a place and locate things using simple prepositions.
Can use indefinite and definite articles correctly with everyday nouns.
concept:
Time to fill a gap: every French noun travels with a little word in front of it — an article — and you've been using them since Unit 1 (le sport, une sœur). Here's how they actually work.

French has two kinds. INDEFINITE articles = "a / some", for one-of-many or first mention: un (masculine), une (feminine), des (plural). un café, une gare, des magasins. DEFINITE articles = "the", for something specific or already known: le (m.), la (f.), l' (before a vowel), les (plural). le café, la gare, l'école, les magasins.

The catch English speakers must accept early: in French the article is almost never optional, and every noun has a gender you simply have to learn with the word. Tip: memorise nouns WITH their article — not « gare » but « une gare ».

To say what exists in a place, use the fixed phrase il y a ("there is / there are") — same form for singular and plural: il y a un parc, il y a des restaurants.

To locate things, use prepositions of place: dans (in), sur (on), sous (under), devant (in front of), derrière (behind), à côté de (next to), près de (near), loin de (far from). The ones ending in de contract with le/les: à côté du parc (de + le), près des magasins (de + les) — but à côté de la gare stays as is.

Describe your own town: « Dans ma ville, il y a… » and place three things with prepositions.

vocabulary.exampleFr: Il y a un parc dans la ville.
vocabulary.exampleEn: There is a park in the town.

vocabulary.exampleFr: Il y a un restaurant ici.
Le chat est dans la maison.
vocabulary.exampleEn: The cat is in the house.

vocabulary.exampleFr: Le livre est sur la table.
vocabulary.exampleEn: The book is on the table.
The station is in front of the hotel.

vocabulary.exampleFr: Le café est à côté de la gare.
vocabulary.exampleEn: The café is next to the station.

examples.fr: Dans ma ville, il y a un parc.
examples.en: In my town, there is a park.

examples.fr: Le café est à côté de la gare.
examples.en: The café is next to the station.

examples.fr: Il y a des magasins dans la rue.
examples.en: There are shops in the street.
The station is in front of the hotel.
